10	/-!
11	# The Quot functor and the relative Grassmannian functor (real definitions)
12	
13	This file replaces the typed-`sorry` bodies of the two headline *definitions*
14	of the A.2.b Quot-scheme chapter (`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`)
15	with real constructions:
16	
17	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.QuotFunctor` (`def:quot_functor`) — the Quot
18	  functor `Quot^{Φ,L}_{E/X/S} : (Sch/S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (u+1)` of `T`-flat, finitely
19	  presented quotients `q : E_T ↠ F` on `X_T = X ×_S T` with proper support
20	  and fibrewise Hilbert polynomial `Φ`, modulo `ker q = ker q'`;
21	* `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Grassmannian` (`def:grassmannian_scheme`) — the
22	  relative Grassmannian functor `Grass(V, d) : (Sch/S)ᵒᵖ ⥤ Type (u+1)` of
23	  rank-`d` locally free quotients of `V_T` on `T`.
24	
25	Universe note: the value category is `Type (u+1)`, not the `Type u` of the
26	iter-176 scaffold — a sheaf of modules on a scheme in `Scheme.{u}` is a large
27	object, so the quotient sets live one universe up.  This is the same forced
28	correction documented for the merged absolute Grassmannian functor
29	(`AlgebraicGeometry.Grassmannian.functor`, `GrassmannianQuot.lean`) and for
30	`picSharp` (`FGAPicRepresentability.lean`); `Functor.RepresentableBy` is
31	universe-polymorphic, so the representability statements are unaffected.
32	
33	## Design
34	
35	A single family of quotients over `T : Over S` is the structure
36	`Scheme.QuotFamily π L E Φ T` (Nitsure §1, "family of quotients of `E`
37	parametrised by `T`"): a sheaf `F` on the relative product
38	`X_T := pullback π T.hom` that is finitely presented
39	(`SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation` — the coherence encoding used by the
40	flattening-stratification input), flat over `T`
41	(`Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat (pullback.snd π T.hom) F`), with proper support
42	(`Scheme.Modules.HasProperSupport`), together with an epimorphism
43	`q : E_T ⟶ F` from `E_T := (pullback.fst π T.hom)^* E`, such that at every
44	`t : T` the graded Hilbert function of the fibre `F_t` twisted by `L_t`
45	*eventually agrees* with `Φ` (`Scheme.hilbertFunction`; by
46	`Scheme.hilbertPolynomial_eq_of_eventually` this eventual-match encoding is
47	exactly the statement "the Hilbert polynomial of `F|_{X_t}` equals `Φ`" of
48	`def:quot_functor`, and it excludes the junk-value coincidence `Φ = 0`).
49	
50	Two families are equivalent when an isomorphism of the targets commutes with
51	the quotient maps (equivalently, `ker q = ker q'` — same convention as the
52	merged absolute Grassmannian `RankQuotient.Rel`).  The functor value at `T` is
53	the quotient by this relation; the morphism action pulls a family back along
54	the induced morphism on relative products `Scheme.quotBaseMap π ψ` (with the
55	`E`-side matched through the pullback pseudofunctor comparisons
56	`Scheme.Modules.pullbackComp` / `pullbackCongr`).
57	
58	The well-definedness of the pullback action rests on four base-change
59	preservation facts (Nitsure §1: "as properness and flatness are preserved by
60	base-change, and as tensor-product is right exact, the pull-back of
61	`⟨F, q⟩` … is well-defined"):
62	
63	1. surjectivity: pullback is a left adjoint, hence preserves epimorphisms
64	   (proved inline);
65	2. finite presentation: `Scheme.Modules.pullback_isFinitePresentation`
66	   (right-exactness of pullback; the per-slice transport of
67	   `Cohomology/PullbackQuasicoherent.lean` with finiteness tracking);
68	3. flatness: `Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat.of_isPullback` (Stacks 01U9 lifted to
69	   the sheaf predicate);
70	4. proper support: `Scheme.Modules.HasProperSupport.of_isPullback`
71	   (properness is stable under base change; the schematic support of the
72	   pullback closed-immerses into the base change of the schematic support);
73	5. fibrewise Hilbert polynomials: `Scheme.hilbertFunction_quotBaseMap`
74	   (the fibre of `X_{T'} → T'` at `t'` is the base change of the fibre of
75	   `X_T → T` at `ψ(t')` along the residue extension `κ(ψ t') → κ(t')`, and
76	   `H⁰` of a module with proper support is invariant under base field
77	   extension — flat base change over a field).
78	
79	Items 3–5 are recorded as named typed `sorry` leaves (their statements are the
80	honest Stacks/Nitsure facts; see the blueprint nodes for sources); items 1–2
81	are proved here.  The functor identities reduce to the pseudofunctor
82	coherence laws of `Scheme.Modules.pullback`
83	(`pseudofunctor_right_unitality` / `pseudofunctor_associativity`), packaged
84	once as the app-level lemmas `Scheme.Modules.pullback_id_app_coherence` and
85	`Scheme.Modules.pullback_comp_app_coherence` and consumed by both functors.
86	
87	## References
88	
89	Blueprint: `def:quot_functor`, `def:grassmannian_scheme`,
90	`thm:grassmannian_representable`, `thm:quot_representable`
91	(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`).
92	Source: [Nitsure], §1 (FGA Explained Ch. 5, arXiv:math/0504020).
93	-/

1383	/-! ## §1. Coherent-sheaf flatness over a base
1384	
1385	For a morphism `f : X ⟶ S` of schemes and a coherent (more generally:
1386	quasi-coherent) `𝓞_X`-module `𝓕`, the *coherent-sheaf flatness* predicate
1387	`CoherentSheafFlat f 𝓕` captures Stacks 00HB lifted to the sheaf level:
1388	for every point `x ∈ X` the stalk `𝓕_x` is a flat `𝓞_{S, f(x)}`-module
1389	via the composite `𝓞_{S, f(x)} → 𝓞_{X, x} → End(𝓕_x)`.
1390	
1391	Affine-locally (Stacks 00HB equivalence): on an affine open `U = Spec A
1392	⊆ S` and an affine open `V = Spec B ⊆ f⁻¹U` with `𝓕|_V = M̃` for a
1393	finite `B`-module `M`, the condition becomes `Module.Flat A M` with the
1394	`A`-module structure inherited via the ring map `A → B`. The
1395	file-internal encoding uses this affine-local form.
1396	
1397	Blueprint reference: `def:coherent_sheaf_flat`. -/
1398	
1399	/-- A coherent sheaf `𝓕` on `X` is **flat over `S`** for a morphism
1400	`f : X ⟶ S` if, for every affine open `U ⊆ S` and every affine open
1401	`V ⊆ f⁻¹U`, the `𝓞_X`-module of sections `Γ(𝓕, V)` is flat as a
1402	`Γ(S, U)`-module, where the `Γ(S, U)`-action is the restriction of
1403	scalars along the ring morphism `Γ(S, U) → Γ(X, V)` (the `appLE`
1404	morphism of `f`).
1405	
1406	This is the affine-local form of Stacks 00HB (a coherent sheaf is flat
1407	over the base if every stalk is flat over the corresponding base
1408	stalk); the affine-local form and stalk form are equivalent for
1409	*quasi-coherent* sheaves on a noetherian base, which is the regime of
1410	the flattening-stratification theorem.
1411	
1412	iter-177+: the body of subsequent flat-locus theorems may package this
1413	predicate as an instance-driven typeclass `Module.Flat`-style if the
1414	sheafified-tensor infrastructure lands in Mathlib; for the iter-176
1415	file-skeleton it is encoded as a `Prop` quantifier. -/
1416	def CoherentSheafFlat {X S : Scheme.{u}} (f : X ⟶ S) (F : X.Modules) : Prop :=
1417	  ∀ {U : S.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen U) {V : X.Opens} (_ : IsAffineOpen V)
1418	    (e : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U),
1419	    letI : Module Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V) := Module.compHom _ (f.appLE U V e).hom
1420	    Module.Flat Γ(S, U) Γ(F, V)
1421	
1422	end Scheme

3500	lemma annihilator_map_basicOpen (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
3501	    (V : X.affineOpens) (f : Γ(X, V.1)) [Module.Finite Γ(X, V.1) Γ(F, V.1)] :
3502	    (Module.annihilator Γ(X, V.1) Γ(F, V.1)).map
3503	        (X.presheaf.map (homOfLE (X.basicOpen_le f)).op).hom
3504	      = Module.annihilator Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) Γ(F, X.basicOpen f) := by
3505	  haveI := V.2.isLocalization_basicOpen f
3506	  letI : Module Γ(X, V.1) Γ(F, X.basicOpen f) :=
3507	    Module.compHom _ (algebraMap Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f))
3508	  haveI : IsScalarTower Γ(X, V.1) Γ(X, X.basicOpen f) Γ(F, X.basicOpen f) :=
3509	    IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_smul (fun _ _ => rfl)
3510	  haveI := isLocalizedModule_basicOpen F V.2 f
3511	  exact (Module.annihilator_isLocalizedModule_eq_map (Submonoid.powers f)
3512	    (restrictBasicOpenₗ F f)).symm
3513	
3514	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
3515	/-- **The annihilator ideal sheaf agrees with the module annihilator on affine opens**
3516	(`lem:modules_annihilator_ideal`). For a quasi-coherent `F` whose section module `Γ(F, V)` is
3517	finitely generated over `Γ(X, V)` on every affine open `V` (the affine-local finiteness supplied,
3518	for a finite-type `F`, by the G1 base case `lem:gf_qcoh_fintype_finite_sections`), the section over
3519	an affine open `U` of the annihilator ideal sheaf equals the module annihilator of the sections:
3520	`(annihilator F).ideal U = Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))`.
3521	
3522	The forward inclusion `≤` is `annihilator_ideal_le` (always available from `ofIdeals`). The reverse
3523	inclusion is genuinely *global*: `annihilator F = ofIdeals (V ↦ Ann_{Γ(X,V)}(Γ(F,V)))` is the
3524	**largest** ideal sheaf contained in the annihilator family, and its value at `U` reaches the full
3525	`Ann_{Γ(X,U)}(Γ(F,U))` precisely when that family is itself a coherent ideal sheaf — i.e. when it
3526	satisfies `IdealSheafData.map_ideal_basicOpen` at *every* affine open. That coherence is
3527	`annihilator_map_basicOpen`, which needs finite generation of `Γ(F, V)` at each `V`; hence the
3528	`hfin` hypothesis. With it the family assembles into an honest `IdealSheafData I` whose `ideal` is
3529	the annihilator family, and `IdealSheafData.ofIdeals_ideal` gives `ofIdeals I.ideal = I`, whence the
3530	claim at every `U` at once. This mirrors Mathlib's `Scheme.Hom.ker_apply`, which likewise builds the
3531	full ideal sheaf and reads off the affine value (using `QuasiCompact f` as its global hypothesis).
3532	Project-local because `annihilator` is. -/
3533	theorem annihilator_ideal (F : X.Modules) [F.IsQuasicoherent]
3534	    (hfin : ∀ V : X.affineOpens, Module.Finite Γ(X, V.1) Γ(F, V.1)) (U : X.affineOpens) :
3535	    (annihilator F).ideal U = Module.annihilator Γ(X, U.1) Γ(F, U.1) := by
3536	  let I : X.IdealSheafData :=
3537	    ⟨fun V => Module.annihilator Γ(X, V.1) Γ(F, V.1), ?_, _, rfl⟩
3538	  · exact congr($(IdealSheafData.ofIdeals_ideal I).ideal U)
3539	  · intro V f

1038	      Scheme.IdealSheafData.vanishingIdeal Z := by

/-- **Pullback preserves quasi-coherence** (Stacks 01BG, general-morphism case).  For an
arbitrary morphism of schemes `g : Y ⟶ X` and a quasi-coherent `F : X.Modules`, the module
pullback `g^* F` is quasi-coherent.

Quasi-coherence is local (`SheafOfModules.IsQuasicoherent.of_coversTop`): the preimages
under `g` of the quasi-coherence cover of `X` cover `Y`, and over each preimage the
per-slice obligation is `presentationPullbackSliceOfOver`. -/
theorem pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom {Y X : Scheme.{u}} (g : Y ⟶ X)
    (F : X.Modules) (hF : F.IsQuasicoherent) :
    ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).IsQuasicoherent := by
  obtain ⟨⟨q⟩⟩ := hF
  -- The preimages of the quasi-coherence cover of `X` cover `Y`.
  have hcov : (Opens.grothendieckTopology Y).CoversTop
      (fun i => g ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i) := by
    intro W x hx
    obtain ⟨U', fU, hf, hU'⟩ := q.coversTop ⊤ (g.base x) (by trivial)
    obtain ⟨i, ⟨gi⟩⟩ := hf
    refine ⟨W ⊓ (g ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i), homOfLE inf_le_left, ⟨i, ⟨homOfLE inf_le_right⟩⟩,
      ⟨hx, ?_⟩⟩
    exact (leOfHom gi) hU'
  -- Per-slice presentations through the restricted-pullback bridge.
  haveI hslice : ∀ i,
      (((Scheme.Modules.pullback g).obj F).over (g ⁻¹ᵁ q.X i)).IsQuasicoherent := fun i =>

open TensorProduct in
/-- **Mixed-base stability of flatness under module localization.**  Let `B` be an
`R`-algebra, `N` a `B`-module that is flat over `R`, and `g : N →ₗ[B] N'` a
localization of `N` at a submonoid `p ⊆ B`.  Then `N'` is still flat over `R`.

Mathlib's `Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule` treats only submonoids of `R`
itself; here the localization happens on the fibre side.  The proof rewrites
`𝟙 N' ⊗ f` as the `IsLocalizedModule.map` of `𝟙 N ⊗ f` (Mathlib's
`IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor`, with the `IsLocalizedModule.rTensor` instances)
and uses that localization preserves injectivity
(`IsLocalizedModule.map_injective`). -/
theorem _root_.Module.Flat.of_isLocalizedModule_algebra {R B : Type v} [CommRing R]
    [CommRing B] [Algebra R B] (p : Submonoid B) {N N' : Type v} [AddCommGroup N]
    [Module R N] [Module B N] [IsScalarTower R B N] [AddCommGroup N'] [Module R N']
    [Module B N'] [IsScalarTower R B N'] (g : N →ₗ[B] N') [IsLocalizedModule p g]
    [Module.Flat R N] : Module.Flat R N' := by
  rw [Module.Flat.iff_lTensor_injectiveₛ]
  intro P _ _ I
  rw [← TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.coe_lTensor (A := B)]
  rw [← IsLocalizedModule.map_lTensor (S := p) (g := g) (f := I.subtype)]
  exact IsLocalizedModule.map_injective p
    (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.rTensor R I g)
    (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.rTensor R P g)
    (TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.lTensor B N I.subtype)
    (by
      rw [TensorProduct.AlgebraTensorModule.coe_lTensor (A := B)]
      exact Module.Flat.lTensor_preserves_injective_linearMap I.subtype
        Subtype.val_injective)

set_option maxHeartbeats 1200000 in
-- Heartbeat headroom: several `IsLocalizedModule`/scalar-tower instance chains
-- at localized section modules (qcqs section-localization engine).
/-- **Chart-basic core of generic flatness.**  Let `Wj ≤ p ⁻¹ᵁ U₀` be an affine
chart whose section module localized at `g ∈ Γ(S, U₀)` is free over
`Γ(S, U₀)_g` (the output of algebraic generic freeness), and let `c ∈ Γ(X, Wj)`
cut a basic open lying above the base basic open `D(g)`.  Then the sections of
`F` over `D(c)` (presented as any open `O = D(c)`) are flat over `Γ(S, U₀)`.

Route: `D(β) = Wj ⊓ p ⁻¹ᵁ D(g)` for `β := appLE g`; the section restriction
`Γ(F, Wj) → Γ(F, D(β))` is a localization at `powers β` over `Γ(X, Wj)`
(Stacks 01P0) hence at `powers g` over `Γ(S, U₀)` (base-ring descent), so
`Γ(F, D(β))` inherits freeness-hence-flatness over `Γ(S, U₀)` from the
hypothesis; finally `Γ(F, D(β)) → Γ(F, D(c))` is a fibre-side localization,
absorbed by mixed-base stability. -/

import Mathlib
import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GenericFlatnessGeometric

/-!
# Flatness over the base is stable under base change

This file proves the base-change stability of the coherent-sheaf flatness
predicate `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat` (blueprint node
`lem:coherent_flat_base_change`; Stacks 01U9, Nitsure §1): for a cartesian
square of schemes

```
  X' --g'--> X
  |          |
  f'         f
  ↓          ↓
  S' --g---> S
```

and a quasi-coherent sheaf of modules `F` on `X` that is flat over `S`, the
pullback `g'^* F` is flat over `S'`.

## Proof route (affine-local, Stacks 00HB/01U9)

The proof is pure assembly of the per-piece engines of
`AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.GenericFlatnessGeometric`:

* For every point `x ∈ X'` choose an affine `U ⊆ S` around the common image
  `f(g'(x)) = g(f'(x))`, an affine `V ⊆ f⁻¹U` around `g'(x)`, and an affine
  `Ut ⊆ g⁻¹U` around `f'(x)` (`exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset`).  The
  **piece** `W := g'⁻¹V ⊓ f'⁻¹Ut` is an affine open of `X'` containing `x`
  (`isAffineOpen_pullback_piece`: it is the fibre product `V ×_U Ut`).
* On the piece, the pulled-back sections `Γ(g'^*F, W)` are the base change
  `Γ(X',W) ⊗_{Γ(X,V)} Γ(F,V)` with `Γ(X',W) = Γ(X,V) ⊗_{Γ(S,U)} Γ(S',Ut)`,
  so flatness of `Γ(F,V)` over `Γ(S,U)` — the hypothesis at the affine pair
  `(U, V)` — transports to flatness of `Γ(g'^*F, W)` over `Γ(S',Ut)`
  (`flat_section_pullback_piece` = `Module.Flat.of_isPushout` fed with
  `isPushout_appLE_pullback_piece` and the base-change section formula
  `pullback_app_isoTensor_baseMap_sectionLinearEquiv`).
* The pieces cover `X'`, so the affine-locality engine
  `flat_section_of_affine_cover` (Stacks 00HT-style: simultaneous-basic-open
  refinement, mixed-base localization stability, and the
  `Module.flat_of_isLocalized_span` glue) upgrades the chart family to
  flatness at *every* affine pair `(U' ⊆ S', V' ⊆ f'⁻¹U')` — which is the
  definition of `CoherentSheafFlat f' (g'^* F)`.

Quasi-coherence of the pullback is `pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom`
(Stacks 01BG, arbitrary morphisms).

## References

Blueprint: `lem:coherent_flat_base_change`
(`blueprint/src/chapters/Picard_QuotScheme.tex`).
Source: [Nitsure], §1 (FGA Explained Ch. 5, arXiv:math/0504020); Stacks 01U9.
-/

set_option autoImplicit false

universe u

open CategoryTheory

namespace AlgebraicGeometry

namespace Scheme

/-- **Flatness over the base is stable under base change** (Stacks 01U9,
lifted to the coherent-sheaf flatness predicate `Scheme.CoherentSheafFlat`
along a cartesian square).  Affine-locally this is `Module.Flat.of_isPushout`
threaded through the quasi-coherent section calculus: on an affine piece
`g'⁻¹V ⊓ f'⁻¹Ut` of the fibre-product square the pulled-back sections are the
base change of the sections of `F`, and the affine-pair predicate is
affine-local for quasi-coherent modules (Stacks 00HT,
`flat_section_of_affine_cover`). -/
theorem CoherentSheafFlat.of_isPullback
    {X S X' S' : Scheme.{u}} {f : X ⟶ S} {g : S' ⟶ S} {g' : X' ⟶ X} {f' : X' ⟶ S'}
    (sq : IsPullback g' f' f g) (F : X.Modules) (hqc : F.IsQuasicoherent)
    (hF : CoherentSheafFlat f F) :
    CoherentSheafFlat f' ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F) := by
  haveI := hqc
  haveI : ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F).IsQuasicoherent :=
    pullback_isQuasicoherent_hom g' F hqc
  -- Per-point charts: an affine piece of the fibre-product square through the
  -- point, over an affine base chart, with flat pulled-back sections.
  have H : ∀ x : X', ∃ (W : X'.Opens) (Ut : S'.Opens),
      IsAffineOpen W ∧ IsAffineOpen Ut ∧ x ∈ W ∧ ∃ (eW : W ≤ f' ⁻¹ᵁ Ut),
        (letI : Module Γ(S', Ut) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F, W) :=
          Module.compHom _ (f'.appLE Ut W eW).hom
        Module.Flat Γ(S', Ut) Γ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F, W)) := by
    intro x
    -- the two composites to `S` agree on `x`
    have hbase : f.base (g'.base x) = g.base (f'.base x) := by
      have h := congrArg (fun φ : X' ⟶ S => φ.base x) sq.w
      simpa using h
    -- affine `U ⊆ S` around the common image point
    obtain ⟨U, hU, hsU, -⟩ := exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset
      (TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_top (f.base (g'.base x)))
    -- affine `V ⊆ f⁻¹U` around `g'(x)`
    obtain ⟨V, hV, hxV, hVsub⟩ := exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset
      (show g'.base x ∈ f ⁻¹ᵁ U from hsU)
    -- affine `Ut ⊆ g⁻¹U` around `f'(x)`
    obtain ⟨Ut, hUt, hxUt, hUtsub⟩ := exists_isAffineOpen_mem_and_subset
      (show f'.base x ∈ g ⁻¹ᵁ U from hbase ▸ hsU)
    have hUSX : V ≤ f ⁻¹ᵁ U := hVsub
    have hUST : Ut ≤ g ⁻¹ᵁ U := hUtsub
    exact ⟨g' ⁻¹ᵁ V ⊓ f' ⁻¹ᵁ Ut, Ut,
      isAffineOpen_pullback_piece sq hUST hUSX hU hUt hV, hUt, ⟨hxV, hxUt⟩,
      inf_le_right,
      flat_section_pullback_piece sq hUST hUSX F hU hUt hV (hF hU hV hUSX)⟩
  choose Wc Uc hWc hUc hmem eWc hflat using H
  -- assemble: chart flatness on an affine cover gives every affine pair
  intro U' hU' V' hV' e
  exact flat_section_of_affine_cover f' ((Scheme.Modules.pullback g').obj F)
    Wc hWc Uc hUc eWc (fun y => ⟨y, hmem y⟩) hflat hU' hV' e

end Scheme

end AlgebraicGeometry
